title: Avoiding Kharas YouTube copyright claims
wikitext:
[]: Avoiding Khara's YouTube copyright claims?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Tue Jan 12, 2021 2:25 pm I really love making edits and music videos and shitty unfunny memes using NGE and NTE footage to post on my channel, but 9/10 times it'll get claimed and hidden by Khara. Like god damn I just want to use like 2 minutes of footage max bruh, I'm not uploading the whole goddamn franchise. Any tips on how to avoid this without ruining the video with a billion effects to fuck up the detection AI?

[]: Re: Avoiding Khara's YouTube copyright claims?             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Jan 12, 2021 2:32 pm You can't, that's just how youtube does things

[]: Re: Avoiding Khara's YouTube copyright claims?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jan 12, 2021 2:35 pm It's called copyright... And the so-called "fair usage" in some countries' copyright laws is never actually defined.

[]: Re: Avoiding Khara's YouTube copyright claims?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 16, 2021 10:04 am There is some definition of Fair Use in the United States, but it’s (by design) still very broad and unspecific in its goals. I don’t know if Fair Use even exists in Japan; I just know Godzilla’s lawyers (Toho) are worse than he is, and Godzilla’s lawyers now have an invested interest in Shin Eva cases, so be careful with your Eva edits.

Like god damn I just want to use like 2 minutes of footage max bruh, I'm not uploading the whole goddamn franchise. Any tips on how to avoid this without rui
